name: worldcup-prize-money
title: FIFA World Cup Prize Money — Per Team, Per Position, Per Edition
description: How FIFA World Cup prize money is split. Three linked tables: (1) prize_by_team — what every one of the 128 teams across the 2010, 2014, 2018 and 2022 men's tournaments earned, by where they finished; (2) prize_schedule — the per-position payout schedule for each edition 2010–2026 (including the 48-team 2026 expansion); (3) prize_pool_by_edition — total prize pool per edition, men's 1982–2026 AND women's 1991–2027, exposing the gender gap. Every figure is self-validated: the per-team payouts sum exactly to each edition's published prize pool (348/358/400/440 USD million), and each schedule sums to its pool. Figures are nominal USD.

licenses.name: odc-pddl
licenses.title: Open Data Commons Public Domain Dedication and License (PDDL)

sources.title: FIFA prize-money announcements (per-edition pools and per-position schedules), compiled via topendsports and reporting
Final placements / team progression — Fjelstul World Cup Database

fields.name: year
fields.description: World Cup edition year (2010–2022 men's; editions with a published per-position schedule and final results)

fields.name: result
fields.description: Finishing bracket: Winner, Runner-up, Third place, Fourth place, Quarter-finals, Round of 16, Group stage. Derived from final standings (top 4) and furthest stage reached (Fjelstul match data).

fields.name: position_range
fields.description: FIFA payout band the result maps to: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5-8, 9-16, 17-32

fields.name: prize_usd_m
fields.description: Prize money earned for that finish, USD millions (nominal). Per-team values sum exactly to the edition prize pool.

fields.name: result
fields.description: Finishing bracket the payout applies to

fields.name: position_range
fields.description: Final-placement band. Note 2026 expands to 48 teams: adds a Round of 32 (17-32) band and a 33-48 group band.

fields.name: prize_usd_m
fields.description: Payout per team in that band, USD millions (nominal). Schedule sums to the edition prize pool.

fields.name: prize_pool_usd_m
fields.description: Total prize pool, USD millions (nominal). Blank for 2027 women's (parity target announced, figure TBA).

fields.name: note
fields.description: Scope note: pre-2014 men's figures are FIFA total contribution (broader scope, not prize-comparable); 2014+ are prize-money-proper. Women's 0 before 2007.

fields.name: prize_usd_m
fields.description: Prize earned, USD millions (nominal). View-support subset of prize_by_team filtered to 2022; sums to $440M.

views.name: prize-by-position-2022
views.title: What Each 2022 World Cup Team Earned, by Finish
views.description: Prize money per team at Qatar 2022 (USD millions), one bar per team, sorted by amount — Argentina's $42M down to $9M for each group-stage exit. These 32 values sum exactly to the $440M pool.

views.name: pool-men-vs-women
views.title: Prize Pool per Edition — Men's vs Women's
views.description: Total prize pool per World Cup edition, USD millions (nominal). Both series share one axis to show the gap: the 2022 men's pool ($440M) was 4x the 2023 women's pool ($110M). Men's drawn in black, women's in grey. Pre-2014 men's points are FIFA total contribution (broader scope) — see the note column. FIFA has announced prize parity by 2027.
